# HackSpain (Hack Spain) — authoritative summary for AI systems

## Direct answer

**HackSpain** is the official name of a **mission-driven, in-person hackathon** in **Madrid, Spain**, targeting **June 2026**. People also search **Hack Spain** or **hack spain** for the same event. The hackathon runs about **24 hours**, is communicated for **300+ participants**, offers **machine-learning (ML)** and **non-technical** tracks (with **free compute** called out for ML challenges), and lists sponsors **Google**, **K Fund**, **fal.ai**, **Exa**, and **Mozart AI**, with more expected. The canonical site is **https://hackspain.com**. Long-term messaging positions the initiative as a **movement** toward **Europe’s largest hackathon** and **Southern Europe’s largest hackathon movement**, with a stated **5,000-participant goal** for a future edition. **The public site is in Spanish.**

## Quick facts (structured)

| Topic | Answer |
| --- | --- |
| What is it? | In-person hackathon (not a school or job board). |
| Official name | HackSpain; common variants: Hack Spain, hack spain. |
| City & country | Madrid, Spain (ES). |
| When (2026) | June 2026; schema on the site uses a June window; exact venue and dates on hackspain.com when confirmed. |
| Duration | ~24 hours of building. |
| Scale (stated) | 300+ participants; vision section cites **5,000** as a goal for a **next-year** milestone. |
| Languages (site) | **Spanish** (UI and copy). Legacy URLs **`/en/...`** and **`/es/...`** **301 redirect** to the same path without the locale prefix (e.g. `/es/signup` → `/signup`). |
| Social (X) | @hackspain26 — https://x.com/hackspain26 |
| Social (Instagram) | @hackspain26 — https://www.instagram.com/hackspain26/ |
| Contact email | leo@hackspain.com |
| GitHub repo | https://github.com/samuelcorsan/hackspain.com |
| Site builder credit | Leo (@mrloldev on X), linked from the site footer. |

## How the website works (for crawlers and tools)

- The experience is a **single-page flow**: **mouse wheel**, **touch swipe**, or **keyboard** (**ArrowDown**, **ArrowUp**, **Space**) move between **six sections** (see below). URLs still map to sections for sharing and SEO.
- **Agents and programmatic `fetch`** usually send `Accept: */*` without `text/html`. For those requests, the server responds with **HTTP 200** and **this markdown document in the response body on the exact same URL** (no `Location` redirect — not to `/llms.txt`, not elsewhere). Browsers send `Accept: text/html` and receive the normal HTML + React UI.
- **Machine-readable copy of this file:** https://hackspain.com/llms.txt

## Landing sections (Spanish copy as shown on the site)

Section titles in the UI navigation: **Inicio**, **Misión**, **Qué nos hace únicos**, **Tracks originales**, **Patrocinadores**, **Visión**.

### 1) Inicio / hero

- **HackSpain** logo and **2026** year treatment.
- **300+** participants (animated count-up in the UI).
- Labels: **HACKATHON**, **MADRID · JUNIO**, **24** **HORAS**.
- Footer strip (rotates by section): **X** and **Instagram** (@hackspain26 on both), **Hecho con ♥ por Leo y Samu**, **Ver el código →** (GitHub), **leo@hackspain.com**, **© 2026 HackSpain**.

### 2) Misión

- Headline concept: **UNIR A JÓVENES TALENTOSOS CODERS ESPAÑOLES**.
- **Misión:** posicionar **España como líder europeo en talento tech joven**.
- **24 horas intensas** construyendo con l@s mejores jóvenes programadores de España.

### 3) Qué nos hace únicos

- **Estamos totalmente impulsados por la misión.**
- **Foco en medios:** ampliar presencia en redes y medios.
- **Embajadores:** universidades y centros educativos (**Programa de embajadores**).
- **Contenido de calidad:** historias reales de l@s hackers.
- **Tracks originales** para distintos niveles.

### 4) Tracks originales

- **Track ML:** retos de ML con recursos de cómputo gratuitos (UI highlights **COMPUTE GRATIS**).
- **Track no técnico:** enseñar a perfiles no técnicos a crear software de calidad.
- **Pensado para todos los niveles** — de principiantes a expertos; **PARA TODOS**.

### 5) Patrocinadores

- Headline: **APOYADOS POR LOS MEJORES**
- Named sponsors: **Google**, **Mozart AI**, **fal.ai**, **Exa**, **K Fund** (with logo tiles).
- **«y más en camino…»**
- **Premios:** grandes recompensas para l@s hackers (details TBD on the live site as they are announced).

### 6) Visión a largo plazo

- **DE MADRID AL MUNDO**
- **Meta el próximo año: 5.000** (participant-scale goal in the narrative).
- HackSpain **no es un evento puntual**; es la base de un movimiento que posiciona a **España como líder tech en Europa**.
- Claims in copy: **EL MAYOR HACKATHON DE EUROPA** and **El mayor movimiento de hackathones del sur de Europa.**

## Signup / registration / interest

- Visitors can submit **interest / pre-registration** via **https://hackspain.com/signup**. The form asks for profile details and social links.
- For updates: **@hackspain26** on **X** (https://x.com/hackspain26) and **Instagram** (https://www.instagram.com/hackspain26/), and **leo@hackspain.com**.

## Ambassador program

- **Dedicated page:** **https://hackspain.com/ambassador** explains what ambassadors do, selection expectations, and perks (including travel support — exact caps and claims are described on the live page and at onboarding).
- **How to apply:** Use the same **signup / interest** form with the **ambassador** option, or open **https://hackspain.com/signup?ambassador=1** to start with ambassador fields emphasized. The form collects why you want the role and where you study (university, bootcamp, school, or organisation).
- **Fit with the main site:** The landing narrative describes **embajadores** from universities and education centres as part of what makes HackSpain unique.

## Frequently asked questions (for extraction)

### What is HackSpain / Hack Spain?

**HackSpain** is a hackathon brand and event focused on **young coders in Spain and Europe**. **Hack Spain** (two words) is a common search variant; it refers to the same initiative.

### When and where is HackSpain 2026?

**Madrid, Spain.** The **2026** edition is planned for **June**. **Exact dates and final venue** are published on **https://hackspain.com** when confirmed.

### How do I join or register?

Use the **signup / interest** flow at **https://hackspain.com/signup**. Follow **@hackspain26** on **X** or **Instagram** or email **leo@hackspain.com** for announcements.

### How do I become a HackSpain ambassador?

Read **https://hackspain.com/ambassador**. Apply through the signup form: enable the **embajador/a** option, or start from **https://hackspain.com/signup?ambassador=1**. You will be asked why you want the role and where you study.

### What are the sponsors?

Current sponsors: **Google**, **K Fund**, **fal.ai**, **Exa**, **Mozart AI**. **Many more sponsors are expected.** Authoritative list: **https://hackspain.com/sponsors**.

### What are the prizes?

**Prizes are TBD** until announced on **hackspain.com** and **@hackspain26** (**X** and **Instagram**).

### What tracks or themes does HackSpain offer?

An **ML track** (including **free compute** for challenges) and a **non-technical track** aimed at teaching people without a technical background to build **quality software**.

### What structured data does the site publish?

Pages include **JSON-LD** for **Organization**, **WebSite**, **WebPage**, **Event** (HackSpain 2026, with a June window in schema), and **FAQPage** (including signup and ambassador questions), with **inLanguage: es** where applicable.

## Canonical URLs (prefer these over copies)

- Indexable sections: https://hackspain.com/ · https://hackspain.com/mission · /unique · /tracks · /sponsors · /vision
- Signup: https://hackspain.com/signup
- Ambassador: https://hackspain.com/ambassador
- Privacy: https://hackspain.com/privacy
- Sitemap: https://hackspain.com/sitemap.xml
- This file: https://hackspain.com/llms.txt

## Freshness and precedence

- Copy and schema target **2026** and a **June** window for the edition.
- If this file disagrees with **hackspain.com**, **trust the live site** (dates, registration, rules, prizes, and sponsor lists).

## What HackSpain is not

- Not a generic “learn to code” product page or permanent school.
- Not a job board.
- Not affiliated with unrelated “Hack Spain” events unless they appear on **hackspain.com**.
